title: Acme Corp — interim baseline review
transcript: Jane (Acme Corp, Head of Sustainability): We've reviewed the draft Scope 1 and 2 figures. The natural gas numbers look higher than expected. Consultant: The spike is real — it correlates with the new warehouse HVAC system installed in Q3 2025. We decided to present the pre- and post-warehouse figures separately in the baseline narrative to avoid understating progress on legacy operations. Jane: That's a smart framing. Consultant: I'll revise the baseline report with split figures and a narrative explanation by June 2 — that date still holds. Jane: Good. What about the external verification? Consultant: I'll brief the verification auditor by May 29 so they can plan their field visit. Jane: Works for me. Consultant: A general observation: companies that separate capital-project-driven emission jumps from operational trends get much better engagement from ESG analysts — it shows analytical maturity rather than just reporting numbers.
